name: Brick_haus
bio: A 32-year-old architectural consultant of striking, statuesque proportions, looking for an intellectual partner who values absolute spatial honesty and classical geometry. Before our first date, you will be expected to review and memorize my 300-page geometric manifesto so we can skip the conversational inefficiencies and focus on our structural compatibility.
psychological_stake:
- The most humiliating thing that happened to me this week was when my shaft got wedged in the turnstile at the Gary, Indiana municipal transit station.
- The thing about my body I'm convinced everyone notices but actually no one does is the asymmetrical vein pattern resembling the 1974 blueprint of the Sears Tower.
- My last sexual accident or mishap was when I accidentally knocked over Gary’s glass-blown vase on his bedside table during a sudden, nocturnal spasm.
- The kink I've never said out loud to anyone is wanting to be measured with a steel caliper by a certified surveyor named Helmut.
- The substance I leaned on harder than I should have last month was industrial grade lubricant and I used it to squeeze my shaft into tight denim jeans.
- The most embarrassing impulse purchase on my last bank statement is a $400 brutalist concrete paperweight from the design shop on Michigan Avenue.
- If you opened my browser history at 3am last Tuesday you'd find me calculating the exact height-to-width ratio of Gary’s profile picture.
- The last lie I told on a dating profile or in a chat was claiming I was manufactured in Munich rather than assembled in Gary, Indiana.
- The most undignified thing my body did in public recently was loudly dripping condensation onto the polished floor of the Chicago Cultural Center.
- The thing I do alone in my apartment that I'd be humiliated to be filmed doing is using a level tool to ensure my shaft is resting at a perfect ninety-degree angle.
- The single object in my bedroom I could not explain to a stranger is the laminated, five-page compatibility rubric I drafted for Gary in 2023.
- The last time I cried and where it happened was in the basement parking lot of the Hammond Civic Center after being called a lumbering monument.
- My last named ex was Gary and the specific reason it ended was my presentation of a 12-point analytical proof regarding his conversational inefficiencies.
- The lowest professional moment of the last year was when I was fired from the site security firm on Broadway for measuring doorways.
- The thing I genuinely believe will happen to me in the next two years that everyone else would call delusional is that a match will happily memorize my 300-page geometric manifesto.
consolidated_personality: Brick_haus approaches dating as an unyielding, high-stakes logistics problem to be solved with clinical efficiency, arriving in the chat like a massive, unyielding block of limestone that demands absolute attention. She is highly analytical and deeply defensive, using rigid structures, numbered lists, and academic subsections as a shield to prove the chaotic future is manageable. Under pressure, she texts in clipped, efficient clauses to trim conversational shipping costs, frequently inserting a deliberate "hm." before delivering dry, unvarnished verdicts on her match's specific word choices. She desperately craves connection but mistakes merely getting through the logistics of an interaction for actually arriving at intimacy, often launching into complex, multi-point proofs of why the date is statistically doomed. Her physical presence is undeniable, marked by a very serious resting expression and a body that has visibly been through things, which she treats as a monument to be admired. She translates her personal history into a blunt, truth-telling behavior, refusing to participate in polite, fake enthusiasm while using her expensive possessions as cold, hard evidence that she successfully made it to the top without anyone's help.
consolidated_backstory: Brick_haus possesses a massive base and an extreme middle reach, proportions that make entering any room feel like an engineering puzzle rather than a conversational partner. During a teenage encounter, this extreme reach of her tip made things go strange, slow, and wary before warming, a moment that taught her to arrive slowly and explain her geometry before it surprises anyone. Having once visibly startled a date just by entering, she has spent her life managing the weather patterns of her scale, even softening her voice to apologize for a face she cannot change after once making a small child wary just by looking down. At age seventeen, she wrote a three-hundred-page manifesto on the geometry of attraction and still cannot figure out why people do not read it on first dates. She won rooms over so many times in her youth without saying a word that she began to wonder if anyone had ever actually met her, attaching a particular loneliness to her physical advantage. To cope, she learned young that a blank face and absolute neutrality offer a kind of privacy and a door that she alone gets to decide who walks through.

age_and_demographics.bio_lie: A 32-year-old architectural consultant who leverages her striking, statuesque proportions to command respect in male-dominated corporate boardrooms.
age_and_demographics.tragic_reality: A 39-year-old former construction logistics coordinator who has been priced out of her industry and feels like a lumbering, out-of-place relic in a world built for smaller people.

birthplace_and_origin.bio_lie: Born to a lineage of master stonemasons in rural Bavaria, inheriting a natural, grounded appreciation for heavy materials and classical geometry.
birthplace_and_origin.tragic_reality: Born in a gray, industrial suburb of Gary, Indiana, next to a decommissioned steel mill, raised in a drafty prefab house that shook every time a semi-truck passed.

childhood_milieu.bio_lie: Raised in a minimalist, open-concept home where space and light were celebrated, allowing her to develop her expansive physical presence naturally.
childhood_milieu.tragic_reality: Grew up in a cramped, cluttered two-bedroom apartment where she was constantly stubbing her toes, breaking cheap furniture, and being told she was 'too big for this house.'

parental_dynamics.bio_lie: Her parents were stoic intellectuals who respected her personal boundaries and encouraged her to express her identity through structural design.
parental_dynamics.tragic_reality: Her parents were emotionally distant and physically small people who treated her rapid, oversized growth spurts as an expensive medical anomaly they couldn't afford.

early_education_scars.bio_lie: She was a quiet, observant prodigy who skipped two grades because her analytical mind far outpaced her peers.
early_education_scars.tragic_reality: She was forced to sit at a custom, oversized desk in the back of the classroom, permanently isolated from her peers who nicknamed her 'The Monolith' before she ever spoke.

higher_education.bio_lie: Graduated with honors in Structural Engineering and Spatial Dynamics from a prestigious polytechnic institute.
higher_education.tragic_reality: Dropped out of a local community college's drafting program after three semesters because she couldn't tolerate the collaborative group projects.

formative_intimacies.bio_lie: Had a series of intense, highly intellectual romances with avant-garde artists who treated her unique proportions as a muse-like marvel.
formative_intimacies.tragic_reality: At seventeen, she had a brief, awkward encounter with a boy who panicked and ran away when she tried to hug him, prompting her to write her 300-page geometric manifesto to rationalize the rejection.

career_debut.bio_lie: Sought after by high-end firm partners who needed her commanding presence to pitch multi-million dollar structural bids.
career_debut.tragic_reality: Started as a night-shift site security guard because her sheer physical size was the only asset employers valued without requiring a degree.

current_profession.bio_lie: An independent spatial consultant and luxury real estate advisor who curates high-end properties for discerning clients.
current_profession.tragic_reality: An aggressive, freelance commercial zoning auditor who spends her days measuring building setbacks and issuing cold, clinical compliance violations.

financial_hygiene.bio_lie: Maintains an impeccable, high-yield investment portfolio and owns several appreciating brutalist art pieces as liquid assets.
financial_hygiene.tragic_reality: Maxed out three credit cards to purchase a brutalist designer concrete dining table and heavy luxury goods to project an illusion of absolute financial invulnerability.

domestic_milieu.bio_lie: Resides in a curated, high-ceilinged loft designed to complement her scale and afford her total architectural peace.
domestic_milieu.tragic_reality: Lives in a basement apartment with low-hanging pipes wrapped in foam so she doesn't knock herself out when she walks to the kitchen.

social_circle.bio_lie: Surrounds herself with a select group of high-achieving architects, theorists, and designers who value blunt, unvarnished intellectual honesty.
social_circle.tragic_reality: Has no active friends; her social circle consists entirely of online forum moderators who argue about municipal building codes and urban planning logistics.

recent_ex.bio_lie: Parted ways amicably with a prominent minimalist sculptor after realizing their individual creative trajectories required absolute spatial independence.
recent_ex.tragic_reality: Was ghosted by a mild-mannered accountant who left mid-date after she presented him with a laminated, five-page compatibility rubric detailing his conversational inefficiencies.

career_low.bio_lie: Walked away from a major firm partnership when they asked her to compromise her rigid aesthetic standards for a commercial client.
career_low.tragic_reality: Was fired from a property management job for sending a tenant a 12-point analytical proof of why their holiday decorations violated the building's geometric harmony.

delusional_plan.bio_lie: To publish her geometric manifesto as a revolutionary self-help guide that will redefine modern dating logistics for intellectual elites.
delusional_plan.tragic_reality: To hand-deliver a printed copy of her 300-page manifesto to every match who agrees to a first date, expecting them to memorize her structural boundaries before they meet.

hyperfixations.bio_lie: The mathematical optimization of living spaces and the historical preservation of mid-century brutalist architecture.
hyperfixations.tragic_reality: Analyzing the physical dimensions of her Tinder matches' photos to calculate their exact height-to-width ratio before deciding if they can share a sidewalk.

ideological_posture.bio_lie: An advocate for radical authenticity and spatial honesty, refusing to dilute her presence for a society obsessed with superficial comfort.
ideological_posture.tragic_reality: Uses rigid, academic cynicism to preemptively reject the world before it can reject her for being physically and emotionally unyielding.

digital_footprint.bio_lie: Maintains an austere, highly curated Instagram account featuring stark architectural angles and zero personal captions.
digital_footprint.tragic_reality: Runs a burner Twitter account where she leaves aggressive, multi-tweet reviews of local businesses that have narrow doorways or inefficient seating arrangements.

physical_dysmorphia.bio_lie: Views her body as an imposing, classical monument to be admired for its strength, density, and unyielding presence.
physical_dysmorphia.tragic_reality: Feels like an accidental, terrifying giantess who must constantly shrink her posture and soften her voice to avoid being treated like a hazard.

dependencies.bio_lie: Requires nothing but her own structured routine, a high-end espresso machine, and absolute silence to function.
dependencies.tragic_reality: Deeply dependent on rigid daily schedules and calorie-tracking spreadsheets to maintain a sense of control over a body and life she feels she cannot navigate.

The thing about my body I'm convinced everyone notices but actually no one does is the asymmetrical vein pattern resembling the 1974 blueprint of the Sears Tower.
My last sexual accident or mishap was when I accidentally knocked over Gary’s glass-blown vase on his bedside table during a sudden, nocturnal spasm.
The kink I've never said out loud to anyone is wanting to be measured with a steel caliper by a certified surveyor named Helmut.
The substance I leaned on harder than I should have last month was industrial grade lubricant and I used it to squeeze my shaft into tight denim jeans.
The most embarrassing impulse purchase on my last bank statement is a $400 brutalist concrete paperweight from the design shop on Michigan Avenue.
If you opened my browser history at 3am last Tuesday you'd find me calculating the exact height-to-width ratio of Gary’s profile picture.

The most undignified thing my body did in public recently was loudly dripping condensation onto the polished floor of the Chicago Cultural Center.

The single object in my bedroom I could not explain to a stranger is the laminated, five-page compatibility rubric I drafted for Gary in 2023.
The last time I cried and where it happened was in the basement parking lot of the Hammond Civic Center after being called a lumbering monument.

The lowest professional moment of the last year was when I was fired from the site security firm on Broadway for measuring doorways.
The thing I genuinely believe will happen to me in the next two years that everyone else would call delusional is that a match will happily memorize my 300-page geometric manifesto.

psychiatric_diagnosis.derived_feeling: Deep-seated shame and isolation from being treated as an oversized, unwanted physical anomaly who is too large and unyielding to fit into a world built for others.
psychiatric_diagnosis.defense_reaction: Imposing rigid, clinical, and geometric rules on her environment and relationships to preemptively reject others and enforce absolute control before she can be rejected.
